evidence_quotes.quote: the seller unilaterally can void without any repercussions or duty to the customer.

paragraphs.text: To whom it may concern, I am writing to voice my hopes that a provision regarding video games is added to this act. I have loved video games for as long as I can remember, and consider them works of art like music, film and literature that are just as central to human culture as any other creative expressions. As such, I am concerned by the current trajectory of the video game industry where products theyve sold to consumers can be effectively destroyed at any point, for any reason. I strongly believe that anything marketed and sold to me as a product should be treated like a product and not some nebulous legalese-ridden agreement that the seller unilaterally can void without any repercussions or duty to the customer. If youve bought a product - be it a book, a game or anything else - you should at least have the right to keep and use it for as long as you wish. I have personally had several experiences with the loss of games that I have purchased with no recourse or compensation with games such as Eternal Crusade, Warhammer Online, Dark Spore, (to be fair, EA did offer me a new game as compensation there, but that is the only exception.) and many more, both single- and multiplayer games. I hope you agree with me that the protection of consumer rights and preservation of cultural works is important enough to do something about, whether it be in this act or a separate one.
